title: ACP server uses unstable Duration::from_mins on stable Rust
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/acp.rs:424
## Violation
`client_request` awaits a `oneshot::Receiver` under:

```rust
match tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_mins(5), rx).await {
```

`Duration::from_mins` is a nightly-only API (unstable feature `duration_constructors`). Compilation against stable Rust fails outright. If the project is currently compiling, it is being silently built with a nightly toolchain — meaning the ACP binary shipped to users without nightly will not exist. If a `rust-toolchain.toml` is masking this, the choice is undocumented in-file and any future stable-only consumer (a packager, a CI runner, a downstream crate) breaks unannounced.

This is also a contract issue: a five-minute hard timeout is hard-coded into a transport helper that has no awareness of the operation it's gating. Token refresh, file read, and a 200-line bash run all share the same five-minute budget with no way to override per call site.
## Mandated refactor
Replace `Duration::from_mins(5)` with `Duration::from_secs(5 * 60)` immediately. Promote the timeout to a named constant (`const CLIENT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(300);`) and accept it as an optional parameter on `client_request` so caller-specific budgets (fast `fs/read_text_file`, long `terminal/wait_for_exit`) can supply their own. Pin a `rust-toolchain.toml` to `stable` and add `cargo +stable build` to CI to catch nightly-only regressions.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
